description_long: This object is a long, cylindrical missile or bomb with a pointed nose cone and a set of four stabilizing fins at both the front and rear ends. The body is primarily metallic gray, with two blue bands and one black band encircling the midsection, likely for identification or safety marking. The nose is white and smoothly transitions into the main body, while the rear features a dark, hollow exhaust or propulsion opening. The fins are sharply angled and symmetrically arranged, providing aerodynamic stability. The surface appears hard and smooth, with a slight sheen, indicating a metal construction. There are no visible buttons, wires, or external detonation mechanisms, and no warning labels or timer displays are present.
description: A sleek, metallic gray missile or bomb with blue and black bands, pointed white nose, and four stabilizing fins at each end. The rear features a dark exhaust opening.
description_view_0: The bomb is seen head-on, showing the pointed white nose and the symmetrical arrangement of the front fins.
description_view_1: A side profile reveals the full length, blue and black bands, and both sets of fins, with the white nose and dark rear exhaust visible.
description_view_2: The rear view highlights the dark, hollow exhaust opening and the angular arrangement of the rear fins.
description_view_3: Another side profile, similar to view 1, showing the bomb's length, bands, and fin placement from the opposite side.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: No visible detonation mechanism; detonation is likely internal or remotely triggered, as there are no external buttons, switches, or wires.
